selected_need: AOF needs a context-efficiency layer that lets humans and AI discover the right CLI commands without repeatedly reading the full CLI reference.
intended_user_or_segment: Self-hosting AOF operators and AI orchestration sessions using the CLI under context constraints.
context_summary: The CLI surface is capable and fairly coherent, but command-role classification is implicit. This creates repeated context cost during operation and review.
hypothesis: A canonical command taxonomy plus command registry artifact, referenced from the AI recognition packet, will improve command selection speed and reduce redundant context loading without changing command semantics.
rejected_alternatives: Add more commands before clarifying command-surface routing.
Rely on the full CLI reference as the primary AI packet surface for every session.
Rename large parts of the command surface before proving taxonomy value.
explicit_risks: Registry drift if command metadata and actual CLI surface diverge.
Taxonomy labels that are too coarse to guide real command choice.
Context-efficiency work could become duplicate prose instead of canonical routing metadata.
delivery_validation_requirements: Registry must be canonical enough that AI recognition packets can reference it instead of embedding the whole CLI reference.
Top command paths must be discoverable by category.
Existing command semantics and authority boundaries must remain unchanged.
need: Reduce command-discovery context cost while preserving current runtime semantics.
intent: Introduce a context-efficiency layer rather than expand runtime authority or command count first.
context: Post-v3.4 self-hosting runtime; release truthfulness is improved, but operator and AI command routing still lacks a minimal canonical surface.
